name: The Bargain Gate
descriptionSeed: Horned architecture and scrupulous fairness. The Abyssal Choir names what you want — plainly, in public, without the decency of pretending otherwise — and then offers you exactly that at exactly what it costs. Their bargains are perfectly fair. That is the horror: they will not protect you from the price of your own wanting, and they consider protecting you from it the true cruelty. The Bargain Gate is named for the waygate, not the trade. Arriving through it is free; the Abyssals note, without pressing the point, that most people who arrive want something.
encounterFlavor: Choir-brokers who name your appetite in one look; someone paying, correctly, and it is terrible; a bargain being offered to you that you want very badly.
questSeeds: A bargain was fair and a child is paying it
Someone in the Valley has been dealing with the Choir and has not told anyone what they promised

encounterSeeds.hint: An Abyssal will fight you and will offer you terms while doing it

worldPosNote: Ordinary ground within the_descent, on its bearing. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
